title: AGENTS.md
text: starting coding task, run relevant tests check coverage know baseline state. Always run air format . running tests, every R file edit. Tests R/{name}.R go tests/testthat/test-{name}.R. Use devtools::test(reporter = "check") run tests Use devtools::test(filter = "name", reporter = "check") run tests R/{name}.R testing functions automatically load code; don’t need . new code accompanying test. existing tests, place new tests next similar existing tests.

title: AGENTS.md
text: goal 100% file-level test coverage across R source files. editing file, ensure still 100% test coverage. check coverage single file: following files intentionally excluded coverage requirements (associated tests): R/seabirddata-package.R R/data-*.R (data integrity tests, coverage)

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement jonthegeek@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to seabirddata
text: outlines propose change seabirddata.

title: Contributing to seabirddata
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to seabirddata
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed).

title: Contributing to seabirddata
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("jonthegeek/seabirddata", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to seabirddata
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to seabirddata
text: Please note seabirddata project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Getting help with seabirddata
text: Thanks using seabirddata! filing issue, places explore pieces put together make process smooth possible.

title: Getting help with seabirddata
text: Start making minimal reproducible example using reprex package. haven’t heard used reprex , ’re treat! Seriously, reprex make R-question-asking endeavors easier (pretty insane ROI five ten minutes ’ll take learn ’s ). additional reprex pointers, check Get help! section tidyverse site.

title: Getting help with seabirddata
text: Armed reprex, next step figure ask. ’s question: ’s best ask R4DS Online Learning Community Slack. options include Posit Community, StackOverflow. people answer questions. ’s bug: ’re right place, file issue. ’re sure: let community help figure ! problem bug feature request, can easily return report . opening new issue, sure search issues pull requests make sure bug hasn’t reported /already fixed development version. default, search pre-populated :issue :open. can edit qualifiers (e.g. :pr, :closed) needed. example, ’d simply remove :open search issues repo, open closed.

title: Getting help with seabirddata
text: ’ll try look issue soon can, packages maintained volunteers. good reprex particularly important might weeks months initial report start working . can’t reproduce bug, can’t fix !

title: Bird observation records — birds
text: Individual seabird observation records 10-minute census period, covering -sea observations 1969 1990. row represents one species entry within census period links corresponding environmental ship data ships via record_id.

title: Bird observation records — birds
text: tibble 49019 rows 26 variables: bird_observation_id (integer) Identifier bird observation record, corresponding original record order source data. record_id (integer) Record identifier. Links ships$record_id. One observation (record_id 1184009, bird_observation_id 974) corresponding ship record source data. species_common_name (character) original log entry recorded source data. May encode species, age, sex, plumage phase single string. NA indicates census period birds recorded (converted sentinel value "[BIRDS RECORDED]" source data). species_scientific_name (character) Scientific name, derived species_common_name Te Papa staff. May represent species aggregate log entry identified single species. species_abbreviation (character) Abbreviated species name (mostly first three letters genus species), derived species_common_name Te Papa staff. age (ordered) Age class, derived species_common_name. One "juvenile", "immature", "subadult", "adult". wan_plumage_phase (ordered) Wandering albatross plumage phase, derived species_common_name. One "brown", "brown plumage breaking", "white patch wing", "wing patch breaking", "white". plumage_phase (ordered) Plumage phase species wandering albatross, derived species_common_name. One "dark", "intermediate", "light", "white". sex (factor) Sex, derived species_common_name. One "female" "male". count (integer) Total number birds counted observation. 99999 used counts estimated 100,000. n_feeding (integer) Number birds observed feeding (unspecified whether actively passively). 99999 used counts estimated 100,000. feeding (logical) Whether birds observed feeding. n_sitting_on_water (integer) Number birds sitting water. sitting_on_water (logical) Whether birds sitting water. n_sitting_on_ice (integer) Number birds sitting ice. sitting_on_ice (logical) Whether birds sitting ice. sitting_on_ship (logical) Whether birds sitting ship. in_hand (logical) Whether birds held hand (.e., captured). n_flying_past (integer) Number birds flying past. 99999 used counts estimated 100,000. flying_past (logical) Whether birds flying past. n_accompanying (integer) Number birds accompanying ship (flying alongside). accompanying (logical) Whether birds accompanying ship. n_following_ship (integer) Number birds following ship's wake. following_ship (logical) Whether birds following ship's wake. moulting (logical) Whether birds observed moulting. naturally_feeding (logical) Whether birds naturally feeding (.e., feeding ship discards).

title: Bird observation records — birds
text: -Sea Observations Seabirds dataset, Te Papa Tongarewa, Museum New Zealand. https://www.tepapa.govt.nz/learn/research/datasets/sea-observations-seabirds-dataset

title: Bird observation records — birds
text: data built records extracted Te Papa staff -sea bird observation logbooks Captain John Arthur Francis Jenkins (1928–1989) count cards held convenor Australasian Seabird Mapping Scheme. logbooks Captain Jenkins held Archives Auckland War Memorial Museum. species_common_name contains original log entry appeared source data. raw column named "Species common name (taxon [AGE / SEX / PLUMAGE PHASE])", reflecting single entry encode species, age, sex, plumage information together. columns species_scientific_name, species_abbreviation, age, wan_plumage_phase, plumage_phase, sex derived log entry Te Papa staff. occurrence columns (feeding, sitting_on_water, etc.), NA indicates occurrence unknown unrecorded (original value "U" source data).

title: seabirddata: Data of At-Sea Observations of Seabirds — seabirddata-package

title: Ship observation records — ships
text: Environmental operational records 10-minute seabird census period, covering -sea observations 1969 1990. row represents one census period links zero bird observation records birds via record_id.

title: Ship observation records — ships
text: tibble 12310 rows 21 variables: record_id (integer) Record identifier. Links birds$record_id. date (date) Observation date. time (time) Local time start 10-minute count. latitude (double) Decimal latitude (negative values indicate southern hemisphere). longitude (double) Decimal longitude. hemisphere (factor) East/West hemisphere: "E" "W". activity (factor) Ship activity count period. One "steaming, sailing", "dropping trash", "trawling", "oceanography", "potting", "line fishing", "cleaning fish", "stationary", "flying helicopters", "whaling". Recoded numeric codes 1–10 source data. speed (double) Ship speed knots. direction (integer) Ship direction degrees. cloud_cover (ordered) Cloud cover: "clear", "partially cloudy", "overcast". Recoded codes 0–2 source data. precipitation (factor) Precipitation type: "none", "squalls", "fog", "drizzle", "rain", "showers", "snow showers", "continuous snow". Recoded numeric codes source data. wind_speed_class (integer) Wind speed Beaufort scale (0–12). Join beaufort_scale descriptions knot ranges. wind_direction (integer) Wind direction degrees. air_temperature (double) Air temperature degrees Celsius. pressure (integer) Atmospheric sea-level pressure millibars. sea_state_class (integer) Sea state class (0–6). Join sea_states descriptions wave height ranges. sea_surface_temperature (double) Sea surface temperature degrees Celsius. depth (integer) Sea floor depth meters. observer (factor) Name observer, decoded 4-letter code source data. census_method (ordered) Count method: "partial" indicates count lasting less 10 minutes casual observation; "full" indicates complete 10-minute count. Recoded "P" "F" source data. season (ordered) Southern hemisphere season: "summer", "autumn", "winter", "spring". Recorded directly source data (likely derived rather entered directly log book data).

title: Ship observation records — ships
text: -Sea Observations Seabirds dataset, Te Papa Tongarewa, Museum New Zealand. https://www.tepapa.govt.nz/learn/research/datasets/sea-observations-seabirds-dataset

title: Ship observation records — ships
text: Several columns derived source data excluded: original row order (implicit data), month (derivable date), longitude 0–360-degree format (derivable longitude hemisphere), 1-degree grid cell centre coordinates (derivable latitude longitude), sea surface salinity (missing one entry).
